thesis: Batch 2 exploits the GLD defensive regime, adds native passive references for the focused symbols, and keeps one legacy TLT control so the campaign can distinguish a real defensive-asset shift from a one-batch artifact.

benchmark.purpose: Primary passive benchmark for all campaign batches

universe.name: core_us_macro_v2
universe.selection_rule: Focused exploit universe around approved US beta ETFs, leveraged growth, and defensive GLD/TLT/IEF references

carry_forward_findings: QQQ/VOO/GLD dual_momentum with 126-day lookback is the current best family cluster.
GLD is a first-class asset in the current sample and must not be treated as a minor add-on.
TLT and IEF are currently lower-priority defensive legs unless new evidence emerges.
Native passive references are required for heterogeneous universes.

candidates.hypothesis: VOO buy-and-hold is needed as a native passive baseline for the focused batch.
notes: Batch 2 native passive reference.

candidates.hypothesis: QQQ 40/180 + 252-day time stop remains the aggressive trend control.
notes: Carry-forward control.

candidates.hypothesis: GLD 30/150 + 252-day time stop remains the leading single-asset active GLD control.
notes: Carry-forward control.

candidates.hypothesis: QQQ/VOO/GLD 126-day lookback with 10-day rebalance is the new rotation control.
notes: Carry-forward control.

candidates.hypothesis: QQQ/VOO/TLT 126-day lookback with 21-day rebalance stays as the legacy defensive control.
notes: Legacy control.

candidates.hypothesis: SPY 40/180 + 252-day time stop + 12% trailing stop stays as the improved broad-market stop control.
notes: Carry-forward control.
